title: Size accelerator memory for the worst concurrent batch
description: Provision and cap memory for the largest batch and longest sequences that can occur together, not the average request.
body: Use when configuring batch limits and instance size for serving. Do not size memory for the typical request — the OOM happens at peak concurrency with the longest inputs (and, for generative models, the largest KV cache). Actions: compute worst-case memory as max-batch times max-sequence footprint plus model weights, set batch caps so that fits, and reject or queue beyond capacity rather than OOM. Validation signals include no OOM under a peak-concurrency stress test and headroom on the memory graph. Risk boundary: an OOM crashes the whole server and drops every in-flight request; treat peak-batch memory as a hard cap, not a soft target.
